title: HTML Security Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential tags and attributes for building a more secure web application.

questions.question: What is 'Cross-Site Scripting' (XSS)?
options: A script that automatically translates a page into other languages.
An attack where malicious code is injected into a web page and executed by users.
A method of cross-site request forgery through a link.
A way to share scripts across different domains.

questions.question: Which JavaScript property should you use to prevent XSS instead of using 'innerHTML'?
options: textContent
outerHTML
value
style

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of adding the 'sandbox' attribute to an <iframe>?
options: It makes the iframe load faster.
It places extraordinary restrictions on the content inside, preventing it from running scripts or accessing cookies.
It ensures the iframe is displayed in high resolution.
It automatically resizes the iframe for different screens.

questions.question: When opening a link in a new tab (target='_blank'), which attribute should you add for security?
options: rel='noopener'
rel='async'
rel='external'
rel='secure'

questions.question: The 'Content Security Policy' (CSP) is primarily used for which purpose?
options: To define the page's color scheme and fonts.
To tell the browser which sources of scripts, styles, and images are trusted.
To set the page's meta description for search engines.
To encrypt the data sent through a form.
